title: Resolve apiv2 bindings test failures
body:
V2 bindings tests were failing because of changes introduced in commit
a2ad5bb.

Signed-off-by: Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@fedoraproject.org>

@rhatdan @baude @jwhonce @vrothberg PTAL.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe*, *lsm5* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
There are a few other maps below this one that need to be initialized as well IIUC. I can add them here itself or in a follow-up PR.
@baude btw, can we enable the bindings tests in CI, so we can immediately catch future failures before merge?
This is breaking the intergeneration tests?

@vrothberg so I tried:
`./bin/podman login registry.gitlab.com --authfile ~/authfile-custom.json`

~/authfile-custom.json populated.

followed by
`./bin/podman logout registry.gitlab.com --authfile ~/authfile-custom.json`

~/authfile-custom.json now empty.

So AFAICT, the test passes locally. Would this be something wrong with the CI env?
@lsm5 did you run it as root?

> @lsm5 did you run it as root?

same result with root
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #6115) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
